story.iWant: the Web interface to work reliably across all major browsers (Chrome, Firefox, Safari)
story.soThat: I can convert presets regardless of my browser choice, with confidence that file upload, WASM execution, conversion, and download all function correctly
story.tasks:
### Task 1: Test File Upload (AC-1) - Prepare Test Environment (install browsers, prepare test files) - Test Drag-and-Drop in each browser - Test File Picker in each browser - Document Results
### Task 2: Test WASM Loading (AC-2) - Test WASM Load Time in each browser - Test WASM Initialization in each browser - Test Error Handling - Document Results
### Task 3: Test Conversion Execution (AC-3) - Test Conversion Success (6 conversion paths × 6 browsers = 36 tests) - Privacy Validation (CRITICAL - zero network requests) - Test Error Handling - Document Results
### Task 4: Test File Download (AC-4) - Test Download Trigger in each browser - Test File Integrity in each browser - Test User Controls - Document Results
### Task 5: Test UI Rendering (AC-5) - Test Layout at multiple screen sizes in each browser - Test CSS Rendering - Test Font Rendering - Use DevTools Responsive Mode - Document Results
### Task 6: Document Browser Compatibility (AC-6) - Create docs/browser-compatibility.md - Document Test Environment - Create Compatibility Matrix - Document Browser Market Share - Document Unsupported Browsers - Document Known Issues
### Task 7: Implement Unsupported Browser Detection (AC-7) - Add Browser Detection to web/main.js - Add CSS Styling for Unsupported Browser Message - Test in Unsupported Browsers - Test in Supported Browsers
### Task 8: Validate Privacy Across Browsers (AC-8) - Setup Privacy Testing for each browser - Test Zero Network Requests (CRITICAL) - Test Local Storage - Test for Tracking Scripts - Test Service Worker (if implemented) - Document Privacy Validation
### Task 9: Update Project Documentation - Update README.md with browser support section - Update Web Interface (web/index.html) - Update Landing Page

story-context.acceptanceCriteria:
### AC-1: File Upload Functionality (All Browsers) - Drag-and-drop works (zone highlights, format detection) - File picker works (native picker, file loads) - File validation (invalid files, corrupted files, size limits) - Tested in ALL supported browsers: Chrome (131, 130), Firefox (132, 131), Safari (18.1, 18.0) ### AC-2: WASM Loading and Initialization - WASM Detection (browser supports WebAssembly MVP) - Load Time (under 3s first visit, under 500ms cached) - Initialization (exports available, memory accessible, no console errors) - Error Handling (load failure, network timeout, memory allocation) - Tested in ALL supported browsers
### AC-3: Conversion Execution (All Browsers) - Conversion Succeeds (completes without errors, under 2s) - Progress Indication (loading indicator, success notification) - Error Handling (invalid format, conversion failure, WASM crash) - Privacy Validation (CRITICAL: ZERO network requests during conversion) - Tested in ALL browsers, all conversion paths (NP3↔XMP, XMP↔LRT, NP3↔LRT)
### AC-4: File Download Functionality - Download Triggers (automatic prompt, correct filename) - File Integrity (valid file, correct size, correct MIME type) - User Control (can cancel, can rename, can choose location) - Tested in ALL supported browsers (Blob download works)
### AC-5: UI Rendering and Responsiveness - Layout Correct (all elements visible, no clipping) - Responsive Breakpoints (desktop 1024px and above, tablet 768-1023px, mobile under 768px) - CSS Rendering (styles applied, custom properties work, layouts consistent) - Font Rendering (system fonts load, text legible, fallbacks work) - Tested in ALL browsers at multiple screen sizes
### AC-6: Browser Compatibility Matrix Documentation - Documentation exists: docs/browser-compatibility.md created - Compatibility Matrix includes browser/version/OS/test results - Known Issues Documented - Market Coverage Calculated (target: ≥90% browser market coverage)
### AC-7: Unsupported Browser Detection - Detection Logic (checks WebAssembly, FileReader, Blob support) - User Message (clear "Unsupported Browser" message, lists supported browsers) - Graceful Degradation (page doesn't crash, UI disabled) - Tested in unsupported browsers (IE11)
### AC-8: Privacy Validation Across Browsers - Zero Network Requests During Conversion (monitored via DevTools) - No Tracking (no analytics, no telemetry, no external resources) - Local Storage Check (no file data stored) - Service Worker (only caches WASM, no data exfiltration) - Tested in ALL supported browsers, documented in browser-compatibility.md

doc.title: Epic 6 - Validation and Testing Technical Specification
doc.snippet: Verifies Web interface functionality across Chrome, Firefox, Safari (latest 2 versions) covering 90%+ browser market. Tests FileReader API, WASM execution, file download across browsers. Manual testing with 3 browsers sufficient for MVP.

doc.title: Product Requirements Document
doc.snippet: Browser Compatibility validates NFR-5 (90%+ browser market coverage). Privacy validation confirms NFR-2 (zero data exfiltration). FileReader API, WASM execution, file download tested across platforms. Cross-browser consistency ensures reliability.

doc.title: Story 2-1: HTML Drag-Drop UI
doc.snippet: Establishes core web interface structure with drag-drop zone, file upload handling, and responsive design. Provides foundation for cross-browser testing.

doc.title: Story 2-2: File Upload Handling
doc.snippet: Implements FileReader API for reading file data as Uint8Array. Uses drag-and-drop events (dragenter, dragover, dragleave, drop) and file picker fallback. Critical for browser compatibility testing.

doc.title: Story 2-6: WASM Conversion Execution
doc.snippet: Implements WebAssembly binary loading, initialization, and conversion execution. Validates WASM support detection and error handling. Performance target under 100ms conversion time.

doc.title: Story 2-7: File Download Trigger
doc.snippet: Implements Blob download using URL.createObjectURL and download attribute. Triggers browser download prompt with correct filename and MIME type. Critical for cross-browser download testing.

doc.title: Story 2-8: Error Handling UI
doc.snippet: Implements comprehensive error handling with global error boundaries, XSS prevention, and accessibility. Includes browser compatibility detection (WebAssembly, FileReader, Blob support) with graceful degradation for unsupported browsers.

doc.title: Story 2-9: Privacy Messaging
doc.snippet: Validates zero network requests during conversion. Confirms Recipe's privacy promise: "Your files never leave your device." Privacy verification critical for cross-browser testing.

artifact.reason: Core web interface structure to test across browsers: drag-drop zone, file upload input, status messages, parameter display, conversion controls, download button, privacy FAQ

Entry point for web interface. Coordinates WASM loading, file upload, format detection, conversion, download. Implements global error handlers and browser compatibility check. Critical for testing WASM initialization across browsers.
WASM loading and initialization logic. Tests WASM binary download, Go runtime setup, and exports availability. Critical for AC-2 (WASM Loading and Initialization).
File upload handling with FileReader API. Implements drag-and-drop events and file picker fallback. Critical for AC-1 (File Upload Functionality).
WASM conversion execution. Calls WASM exports to convert file data. Critical for AC-3 (Conversion Execution) and AC-8 (Privacy Validation - zero network requests during conversion).
File download implementation using Blob and URL.createObjectURL. Critical for AC-4 (File Download Functionality).
Browser compatibility detection (WebAssembly, FileReader, Blob support). Implements unsupported browser messaging. Critical for AC-7 (Unsupported Browser Detection).

artifact.symbol: Responsive layout and browser compatibility styles
artifact.reason: CSS rendering tests: responsive breakpoints, custom properties, Flexbox/Grid layouts, font rendering. Critical for AC-5 (UI Rendering and Responsiveness).
Sample file for cross-browser testing. Used to validate file upload, format detection, conversion, and download across all supported browsers.
Sample file for cross-browser testing. Validates XML parsing and generation across browsers.
Sample file for cross-browser testing. Validates Lua parsing and generation across browsers.
Existing Go test infrastructure validates conversion correctness. Browser tests validate same functionality in WASM environment across different browsers.
NP3 format parser/generator tests. Browser tests validate same logic compiled to WASM works identically across Chrome, Firefox, Safari.

frontend.vanilla-js: No npm packages - pure ES6 modules
frontend.web-apis: FileReader API, Blob API, URL.createObjectURL, drag-and-drop events

wasm.build-target: GOOS=js GOARCH=wasm go build -o web/recipe.wasm cmd/wasm/main.go

devtools.console-tab: Check JavaScript errors, WASM load status
devtools.responsive-mode: Test responsive breakpoints and mobile layouts

constraints.constraint: Manual testing approach required for browser compatibility. Automated E2E testing (Playwright, Selenium) is out of scope for MVP. Test matrix requires systematic manual verification across 6 browser configurations.

Focus on latest 2 versions of Chrome, Firefox, Safari only. Edge (Chromium-based) is optional bonus. Mobile browsers (iOS Safari, Chrome Mobile) are secondary priority. No IE11 support required (unsupported browser message acceptable).

CRITICAL: Privacy validation (zero network requests during conversion) must be manually verified in ALL supported browsers using DevTools Network tab. This is non-negotiable for Recipe's privacy promise.
WebAssembly MVP support is minimum requirement. Browsers without WASM support show clear "unsupported browser" message. No polyfills or fallbacks for WASM.
Zero external test dependencies. No npm packages for testing. No cloud testing services (BrowserStack, Sauce Labs). All testing uses browser DevTools (built-in).
All test results must be documented in docs/browser-compatibility.md with compatibility matrix, known issues, market coverage calculation. Documentation is deliverable, not optional.
Primarily documentation and validation story. Minimal code changes (browser detection in main.js, unsupported browser styles in style.css). No new features implemented.
Minimum 36 conversion tests (6 conversion paths × 6 browsers). Plus 5 feature tests (Upload, WASM, Convert, Download, UI) × 6 browsers = 66+ total tests. Systematic coverage required.
Safari testing requires macOS. Windows/Linux developers cannot test Safari. Team must have access to macOS device or accept Safari test gaps.

interface.name: FileReader API
interface.notes: Used for reading uploaded file as Uint8Array. Must work identically in Chrome, Firefox, Safari. Critical for AC-1 (File Upload).

interface.name: Drag and Drop Events
interface.notes: Drag-and-drop file upload. Browser-specific quirks possible (Safari drag events differ). Critical for AC-1 (File Upload).

interface.name: WebAssembly Loading
interface.notes: WASM binary loading and Go runtime initialization. Must detect WASM support (typeof WebAssembly !== 'undefined'). Critical for AC-2 (WASM Loading) and AC-7 (Unsupported Browser Detection).

interface.name: WASM Conversion Exports
interface.notes: WASM conversion function called from JavaScript. Must work identically across all browsers. Critical for AC-3 (Conversion Execution).

interface.name: Blob Download API
interface.notes: File download without server. Uses Blob and URL.createObjectURL. Browser-specific download behavior (Safari may differ). Critical for AC-4 (File Download).

interface.name: Browser Feature Detection
interface.notes: Detects required browser features. Shows "unsupported browser" message if any feature missing. Critical for AC-7 (Unsupported Browser Detection).

interface.name: CSS Custom Properties (Variables)
interface.notes: CSS variables for theming. Must work in all modern browsers (IE11 unsupported). Critical for AC-5 (UI Rendering).

interface.name: Flexbox and Grid Layouts
interface.signature: .container { display: flex; flex-direction: column; } .grid { display: grid; grid-template-columns: repeat(auto-fit, minmax(250px, 1fr)); }
interface.notes: Modern CSS layout systems. Browser support excellent (Chrome, Firefox, Safari). Critical for AC-5 (UI Rendering and Responsiveness).

tests.standards: Manual browser compatibility testing following systematic test matrix approach. All tests executed manually using browser DevTools for validation. No automated E2E testing framework required for MVP. Test documentation uses structured compatibility matrix format with pass/fail/caveat indicators. Privacy validation uses Network tab monitoring to confirm zero network requests during conversion (critical requirement). Results documented in docs/browser-compatibility.md with browser version, OS, feature test results, and known issues.

test.name: File Upload - Drag and Drop (All Browsers)
test.approach: Manual: Open Recipe in each browser (Chrome 131/130, Firefox 132/131, Safari 18.1/18.0). Drag testdata/np3/portrait.np3 over drop zone. Verify zone highlights. Drop file. Verify format detection executes. Verify file loaded successfully. Repeat with invalid file (wrong format) and verify error message. Document results in compatibility matrix.
test.expected: Drag-and-drop works identically in all 6 browsers. Zone highlights on dragover. File loads on drop. Format detection executes. Error handling graceful for invalid files.

test.name: File Upload - File Picker (All Browsers)
test.approach: Manual: Click drop zone in each browser. Verify native file picker opens. Select testdata/xmp/portrait.xmp. Verify file loads successfully. Verify format detection executes. Test with invalid file (corrupted) and verify error message. Document browser-specific quirks (Safari file picker UI differs).
test.expected: File picker works in all browsers. File loads successfully. Format detection executes. Error messages clear and consistent.

test.name: WASM Load Time and Caching (All Browsers)
test.approach: Manual: Open Recipe in each browser with DevTools Network tab open. First visit (clear cache): Monitor recipe.wasm download time (target under 3 seconds). Refresh page (cached): Verify WASM loads from cache (target under 500ms). Document load times for each browser. Note any browser-specific caching behavior.
test.expected: WASM loads under 3 seconds first visit. Cached loads under 500ms. All browsers cache WASM binary correctly. No console errors during load.

test.name: WASM Initialization and Exports (All Browsers)
test.approach: Manual: Open browser DevTools Console. After WASM loads, check: typeof WebAssembly (should be 'object'). typeof window.convertPreset (should be 'function'). Verify no JavaScript errors in console. Test WASM memory accessible via console commands. Document initialization success/failure per browser.
test.expected: WASM initializes successfully in all browsers. convertPreset function available globally. No console errors. Memory accessible.

test.name: Conversion Execution - All Paths (All Browsers)
test.approach: Manual: For each browser, test all 6 conversion paths: NP3→XMP, XMP→NP3, XMP→lrtemplate, lrtemplate→XMP, NP3→lrtemplate, lrtemplate→NP3. Upload file, select target format, click Convert. Verify conversion completes without errors (under 2 seconds). Verify success notification displays. Total: 6 browsers × 6 paths = 36 conversion tests. Document failures or slowness.
test.expected: All 36 conversion tests pass. Conversion completes under 2 seconds. No errors. Success notifications consistent across browsers.

test.name: Privacy Validation - Zero Network Requests (CRITICAL)
test.approach: Manual: CRITICAL TEST. For each browser, open DevTools Network tab. Clear network log. Upload file. Click Convert. MONITOR NETWORK ACTIVITY CLOSELY. Verify ZERO network requests during conversion (only WASM execution). Screenshot network tab showing zero requests. Repeat for all 6 browsers. Document privacy validation in browser-compatibility.md with screenshots.
test.expected: ZERO network requests during conversion in all browsers. Privacy promise validated: "Your files never leave your device." Screenshots confirm zero network activity.

test.name: File Download - Blob Download (All Browsers)
test.approach: Manual: Complete conversion in each browser. Click Download button. Verify browser download prompt appears. Verify file downloads to default folder. Verify filename correct (original name with new extension). Open downloaded file in target application (Lightroom for XMP, NX Studio for NP3). Verify file valid and correct. Document browser-specific download behavior (Safari downloads.html issue, etc.).
test.expected: Download works in all browsers. File valid and correct. Filename accurate. Browser download prompts consistent (or document differences).

test.name: UI Rendering - Multiple Screen Sizes (All Browsers)
test.approach: Manual: Open Recipe in each browser. Test at 1920x1080 (desktop): Verify all UI elements visible, drop zone sized appropriately, buttons accessible. Test at 1366x768 (laptop): Verify layout adjusts. Test at 768x1024 (tablet portrait): Verify layout functional. Test at 375x667 (mobile): Verify basic functionality. Use DevTools Responsive Design Mode for size testing. Screenshot each breakpoint in each browser. Document rendering issues.
test.expected: UI renders correctly at all sizes in all browsers. Responsive breakpoints work. No clipping, no overflow. Consistent layout across browsers.

test.name: CSS Rendering Consistency (All Browsers)
test.approach: Manual: Visual inspection in each browser. Verify styles applied correctly (no browser-specific CSS bugs). Verify custom properties (CSS variables) work. Verify Flexbox/Grid layouts consistent. Verify font rendering (system fonts load, text legible, fallbacks work). Screenshot UI in each browser for comparison. Document CSS inconsistencies.
test.expected: CSS renders identically (or document minor differences). Custom properties work. Layouts consistent. Fonts render correctly.

test.name: Documentation - Compatibility Matrix
test.approach: Create docs/browser-compatibility.md. Document test environment (test date, Recipe version, test files, platforms). Create compatibility matrix table with browser/version/OS/test results (Upload, WASM, Convert, Download, UI, Overall). Calculate browser market share (cite StatCounter). Document known issues (if any). Document unsupported browsers (IE11, etc.).
test.expected: Documentation comprehensive. Compatibility matrix complete with all test results. Market coverage calculated (target 90%+). Known issues documented.

test.name: Unsupported Browser Detection - IE11
test.approach: Manual: Open Recipe in IE11 (if accessible). Verify checkBrowserSupport() function detects missing WebAssembly support. Verify "Unsupported Browser" message displays. Verify message includes list of supported browsers and download links. Verify page doesn't crash. Test in very old Chrome/Firefox versions if accessible. Document detection behavior.
test.expected: Detection works in IE11. Clear "Unsupported Browser" message. Page doesn't crash. Supported browsers pass detection.

test.name: Browser Detection Implementation
test.approach: Code review: Verify checkBrowserSupport() in web/static/error-handler.js (or main.js). Verify checks for WebAssembly, FileReader, Blob. Verify showUnsupportedBrowserMessage() displays clear message. Verify supported browsers pass detection (Chrome, Firefox, Safari). Add unit test if possible (jsdom or manual verification).
test.expected: Detection logic correct. All required features checked. Message displays correctly in unsupported browsers. Supported browsers pass.

test.name: Privacy Validation - localStorage and IndexedDB
test.approach: Manual: Open DevTools Application tab (Chrome) or Storage tab (Firefox) in each browser. Upload file and convert. Check localStorage: Verify no file data stored (only filenames if conversion history exists). Check IndexedDB: Verify no file data stored. Document storage inspection results in browser-compatibility.md.
test.expected: No file data stored in localStorage or IndexedDB. Only filenames (if history implemented). Privacy maintained across all browsers.

test.name: Privacy Validation - No Tracking Scripts
test.approach: Manual: Open DevTools Network tab. Load Recipe page. Check for analytics domains (google-analytics.com, mixpanel.com, etc.). Verify NO analytics scripts loaded. Verify NO telemetry or crash reporting. Verify NO external fonts/resources during conversion. Document zero tracking confirmation in browser-compatibility.md.
test.expected: Zero analytics scripts. Zero tracking. Zero external resources during conversion. Privacy validated across all browsers.

test.name: End-to-End Workflow (All Browsers)
test.approach: Manual: Complete full workflow in each browser: Open Recipe → Upload file (drag-and-drop) → Verify format detection → View parameter preview → Select target format → Click Convert → Verify zero network requests → Download result → Verify file valid. Document complete workflow success/failure per browser. Time complete workflow (target under 30 seconds).
test.expected: Complete workflow succeeds in all browsers. Zero network requests during conversion. File valid and correct. Workflow intuitive and fast.
